title: EGPS generator timeout must be loud + distinguishable from missing predicates (cycle-444 false-FAIL)
description:
CYCLE-444 POST-MORTEM (2026-07-02): the cycle FAILED despite LLM-audit PASS (confidence 0.9), adversarial-review MEDIUM-only, all suites green. ROOT CAUSE: TDD wrote heavyweight coverage-floor predicates (acs/cycle444 re-runs `go test -coverprofile` over core/audit/bridge — ~29s runtime WARM, measured 28.8s wall); generateACSVerdict (go/internal/phases/audit/audit.go:430) runs the whole Go lane under acssuite.DefaultTimeout=60s (acssuite.go:40); cold `-tags acs` compile in the fresh worktree + 29s runtime exceeded the ceiling -> context canceled -> acssuite.Run error -> acs-verdict.json never written -> Classify missing-file FAIL floor (audit.go:123-150) overrode the PASS. ~1300 lines of good tests went unshipped; the batch ended after 1 cycle.

IMMEDIATE MITIGATION ALREADY APPLIED (operator, 2026-07-02): .evolve/policy.json acs.go_timeout_s=300. This item is the SYSTEMIC fix (the class, not the instance):
1. DISTINGUISH timeout from absence: when acssuite.Run fails on context.DeadlineExceeded, the diagnostic must name the timeout, the configured ceiling, and the fix knob (policy acs.go_timeout_s) — and must surface in the DOSSIER defects (cycle-444's dossier said only 'see acs-verdict.json', a file that did not exist — effectively silent).
2. RETRY-ONCE on timeout with a warmed build cache (the first run pays cold `-tags acs` compile; a single retry after `go build -tags acs ./acs/...` separates compile cost from run cost) OR pre-warm the acs build during an earlier phase.
3. CONSIDER: scale the ceiling with predicate count/weight, or let TDD declare an expected-runtime hint in the predicate file that the generator honors (bounded by a hard max).
4. REGRESSION TEST: a predicate suite whose wall time exceeds the configured ceiling must produce a FAIL whose finding names the timeout (not a bare missing-file FAIL); a suite under the ceiling must generate acs-verdict.json as today.

CONSTRAINTS: config-driven (policy acs block), no env flags, TDD red-first, apicover -enforce clean. NOTE the related adversarial-review F1 (cycle-444): apicover-pipeline fail-open WARN — consider the recommended WARN->FAIL escalation when the cycle's own diff broke the tool, as part of this hardening or a follow-up.
